# SecKeychainEventMask

**Structure**

Bit masks corresponding to the events that can trigger a keychain callback.

## Declaration

```swift
struct SecKeychainEventMask
```

## Overview

Bitwise `OR` one or more of these masks together to provide the `eventMask` input to the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecKeychainAddCallback(_:_:_:)] function to indicate what event or events should trigger your callback.

## Initializers

- **init(rawValue:)**: Initializes an event mask value.

## Constants

- **lockEventMask**: If the bit specified by this mask is set, your callback function is invoked when a keychain is locked.
- **unlockEventMask**: If the bit specified by this mask is set, your callback function is invoked when a keychain is unlocked.
- **addEventMask**: If the bit specified by this mask is set, your callback function is invoked when an item is added to a keychain.
- **deleteEventMask**: If the bit specified by this mask is set, your callback function is invoked when an item is deleted from a keychain.
- **updateEventMask**: If the bit specified by this mask is set, your callback function is invoked when a keychain item is updated.
- **passwordChangedEventMask**: If the bit specified by this mask is set, your callback function is invoked when the keychain password is changed.
- **defaultChangedEventMask**: If the bit specified by this mask is set, your callback function is invoked when a different keychain is specified as the default.
- **dataAccessEventMask**: If the bit specified by this mask is set, your callback function is invoked when a process accesses a keychain item’s data.
- **keychainListChangedMask**: If the bit specified by this mask is set, your callback function is invoked when a keychain list is changed.
- **trustSettingsChangedEventMask**: If the bit specified by this mask is set, your callback function is invoked when there is a change in certificate trust settings.
- **everyEventMask**: If all the bits are set, your callback function is invoked whenever any event occurs.

## Conforms To

- BitwiseCopyable
- Equatable
- ExpressibleByArrayLiteral
- OptionSet
- RawRepresentable
- Sendable
- SendableMetatype
- SetAlgebra
